ops.title: Plugin binary installers are brew-first — an installed brew binary wins even when it trails the plugin pin; canonical script lives in repo-bootstrap
ops.body:
For cc plugin binary provisioning, the user wants ONE canonical installer script whose home is cc-skills/plugins/repo-bootstrap (templates/plugin/install-binary.sh), consumed by plugin-template and the fleet via a tiered fetch (local checkout → marketplace clone → raw.githubusercontent pinned to a commit SHA, never floating main). Resolution is brew-first: a brew-installed binary wins outright (symlink to it, best-effort `brew upgrade` if stale — deliberately diverging from cc-notes, which skips brew for pinned requests); if brew is present but the binary absent, `brew install yasyf/tap/<name>` before falling back to a sha256-verified static download into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/bin`. `plugin/bin/<name>` is only ever a symlink (plugin dir is ephemeral; DATA dir is durable).

**Why:** the user explicitly locked brew-first ("keep the brew-first approach") and repo-bootstrap as the canonical home ("cc-interact/plugin-template should not be the canonical place… it should grab it from there") across two plan review rounds.

**How to apply:** never author per-repo installer variants; render from the repo-bootstrap template ([[cc-skills-plugin-source]]) and roll fixes out via `render.sh --sync-scripts`. Don't "fix" brew-lag by preferring the static download.
